# Static Array Extraction — V2 Migration Step 2

*Generated by `registry/extract_static.mjs` (re-run anytime; deterministic).*

## What this is

The ~470-line claim in `REPO_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md` was that "~500 chains of work
are trapped in code." This step lifts that data out of the `src/lib/*.js`
static arrays into versioned, per-family registry files — the first concrete
move toward the **registry-first** V2 architecture.

The extractor replicates `chainNormalizedIndex.js`'s exact merge (first-wins
dedup by `chain_id`, then manufacturer `MERGE_REFS` patched on), so the output
equals what the live app actually builds — then corrects two things the runtime
does silently:

1. **Family keys → canonical enum labels.** Records store internal keys
   (`performance_roller`); the registry stores the real Base44 enum label
   (`Performance Roller Chains`). Nothing is laundered — anything that fails to
   map is parked in `Specialty / Custom Chains` and flagged `needs_review`.
2. **Provenance tagging.** Every record gets `catalog-verified` /
   `standard-nominal` / `derived` based on its source refs and standard.

## Headline numbers

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Chains actually merged by the app** | **304** |
| Family files written | 18 (the full enum) |
| Duplicate `chain_id` collisions in static set | 0 |
| Families that failed to map | 0 |
| Empty families (no static data) | 3 — Steel Bushed, SharpTop, Forged |

- `catalog-verified`: 222
- `standard-nominal`: 47
- `derived`: 35  ← flagged `needs_review`, lowest-confidence; triage first

## Findings that change the plan

1. **The real static count is 304, not ~500.** Fable's estimate counted source
   files the app never loads (see #2). 304 is the true universe that merges into
   the live DB today.

2. **`src/lib/ansiFamilyExpansion.js` is dead data — 73 records, imported
   nowhere.** `chainNormalizedIndex.js` pulls `ansiRollerChainExpansion`
   instead. These 73 chains have never reached the app. **Decision needed:**
   fold them into the registry (re-validate first) or discard them. They are NOT
   included in the 304 above.

3. **Three enum families have zero data** (Steel Bushed, SharpTop, Forged).
   Consistent with the audit's note that Wave F filed Steel-Bushed / pintle-type
   chains under "Engineered Class Chains." The 39 Engineered Class records are
   the prime candidates for re-categorization into these empty families.

4. **No internal `chain_id` collisions** — the static set is internally clean.
   Collisions only happen at runtime when the static set meets the DB, which is
   exactly what the pending live-DB audit (`audit_unikatalog.py`) will surface.

## Output layout

```
registry/
  families/<slug>.json   18 files; { family, slug, count, chains:[...] }
  _manifest.json         counts, provenance, collisions, unmapped list
  extract_static.mjs     reproducible generator
  EXTRACTION_REPORT.md   this file
```

Records are **lossless** — the full static object (incl. `specs`,
`performance_tiers`, `source_refs`, `attachments_available`) is preserved, so
the `/dimensions`, `/performance`, and `/equivalents` sub-registries can be
derived later without touching the source JS again. Added fields are
namespaced: `_origin`, `_family_key`, `_family_unmapped`, `provenance`,
`needs_review`.

## Suggested next steps

1. Triage the **35 `derived`** records — confirm or correct their data.
2. Re-categorize **Engineered Class (39)** into Steel Bushed / SharpTop / Forged.
3. Decide the fate of the **73 orphaned `ansiFamilyExpansion.js`** records.
4. Harden `audit_unikatalog.py` into `registry/validate.py` (the commit gate)
   and point it at these files + the pending live-DB dump.
